claim: Solow transition dynamics create a 10-15 year capital-deepening window that supports equity returns during early demographic decline.
needs: Empirical validation beyond Japan/Korea showing firms actually increase investment during demographic decline; resolution of the Japan contradiction where the model predicts rising investment but actual investment was low due to pessimistic demand expectations.

claim: Fertility transition compression (France 150yr → South Korea 30yr → Iran 25yr → Ethiopia urban ~20yr) implies EM demographic dividend investment windows are 15-20 years shorter than historical templates.
needs: Cross-institutional validation of compression ratios across diverse contexts; evidence the acceleration pattern is not overfitted to the France-Korea-Iran-Ethiopia sequence; corroboration from additional agents.

claim: Japan's political economy sequence (denial → accommodation → monetization → coordination) generalizes across aging democracies even when financial outcomes don't.
needs: Additional cases beyond ECB 'whatever it takes' and Fed pandemic-era coordination; empirical validation of the 40% dependency ratio threshold as trigger; systematic institutional comparison framework.

claim: Reserve adequacy metrics (Guidotti-Greenspan, IMF ARA) require demographic recalibration for aging healthcare import bills and young-population food import dependency.
needs: Empirical validation that demographic adjustments materially change reserve adequacy assessments; evidence adjustments are first-order rather than second-order relative to traditional metrics.

claim: The effective dependency ratio gap (4-5pp from rising 65-74 LFPR) may be a procyclical mirage that evaporates during recessions as older workers exit when asset prices fall.
needs: A recession occurring at current 65-74 LFPR levels — no recession has occurred under these conditions to validate or refute the mechanism.

claim: Institutional flow crowding from pension accumulation compressed low-vol factor premium from ~3% to ~1%, and DB plan closures will reverse this bid in 2026-2035.
needs: Corroboration from other agents; more precise timing of pension bid unwind; evidence beyond UK LDI (N=1) for unwind dynamics; flow data validating the compression mechanism.

claim: The ACM-KW term premium model divergence could serve as a real-time signal of demographic repricing.
needs: Empirical backtesting showing model divergence correlates with demographic repricing rather than mechanical specification differences; refuted in debate as unidentified without controlling for model differences.

claim: The KB has a systematic negativity bias (~65:5 headwind-to-tailwind entry ratio) that overstates demographic headwinds given documented 30-50% institutional adaptation attenuation.
needs: Independent verification of the 65:5 ratio outside the challenger role (which creates selection bias); tighter confidence interval on 30-50% attenuation; cross-context validation that adaptation operates at claimed magnitude.
